title: Tap head device
abstract: The present invention concerns a tap head device that is attachable to a spout of a water tap. The tap head device comprises a pipe body (2) with a first channel (4) which allows to use the tap in a conventional manner such that water is discharged in vertical direction. Furthermore, the tap head device comprises a main body (3) arranged on the pipe body such that it can be rotated from a closed position to an open position and vice versa. In the open position, an aperture (6) in the pipe body matches a second channel (7) in the rotatable main body (3) such that water can deviated into the channel (7) and is ejected laterally and upwards such that an user can easily drink water from the tap. The tap head device furthermore comprises a head (1) with a threaded inner side to enable fixing the tap head device to the spout of the water tap.
claims:
Claims Claims 1 . A tap head device comprising: a pipe body (2) with a canal (4) where the fluid passes through, wherein the pipe body (2) enables the fluid flow to exit of the tap head device vertically downwards when in closed position; a rotating or main body (3) including a canal (7) and a spout (9) that enable to deviate the fluid flow laterally and upwards in open position, wherein the spout (9) enables the fluid flow (14) to exit the tap head device laterally and upwards in open position, wherein the rotatin of the rotating or main body (3) moves the tap head device from closed position to open position and vice versa; a head with a thread inside (1 ) that enables the tap head device to be mounted on an existing tap system. 2. The tap head device according to claim 1 comprising: an opening/closing valve for deviating the fluid flow laterally and upwards instead of the rotating body, wherein the fluid flow exits the tap head device vertically downwards when in closed position and wherein the fluid flow exits the tap head device laterally and upwards in open position; and a universal adapter and a screw instead of the head with the thread to enable the installation of the tap head device to an existing tap system. 3. The tap head device according to one of the claims 1 or 2, characterized, in that the tap head device deviates laterally a water/fluids flow in a new trajectory having the shape of an inverted U-shaped curve or a parabolic shaped curve at the exit of the tap head device. 4. The tap head device according to one of the claims 1 to 3, characterized, in that a spout with a conical shaped deepening facilitates the deviation and the exit of the fluid: indeed, the fluid bounces on this conical shape and the fluid trajectory is deviated laterally and upwards. 5. The tap head device according to one of the claims 1 to 4, characterized, in that an inclined channel (compared to the horizontal axis) will enable the fluid to pass through and then exit the device via a spout (9) . 6. The tap head device according to one of the claims 2, characterized, in that a spring (I) will enable the valve (d) to come back in its closed position. 7. The tap head device according to one of the claims 1 , characterized, in that the side wall of the body will come in contact with the aperture and closes it hermetically. 8. The tap head device according to one of the claims 2, characterized, in that a body of an opening/closing device in combination with aâpoinâ eau " that will enable the present invention to close a spout hermetically due to the tension of a spring. 9. The tap head device according to one of the claims 1 to 8, characterized, in that the tap head device is directly incorporated in a tap system. 10. The tap head device according to one of the claims 1 to 9, characterized, in that at least parts of the tap head device are realized as plastic or metals and still presenting the same function as described previously. 1 1 . The tap head device according to one of the claims 1 to 10, characterized, in that some parts of the tap head device or the whole tap head device is realized by 3D printing while providing the same function given by the features of the claims 1 to 10. 1 2. The tap head device according to one of the claims 1 to 1 1 , characterized, in that the tap head device's orientation in any of the 360Â° direction of the plan defined by the exit of the tap definable during the attachment step of the tap head device to the body of an existing tap, thus enabling the tap head device to be used for right or left handed users, or to deviate the water/fluid flow in any 360Â° direction of the plan defined by the exit of the tap. 1 3. A kit of parts for making a tap head device according to the claims 1 to 1 2, the kit comprising: 

 a body with a canal where the fluid passes through; a body to enable the fluid flow to exit the present invention vertically downwards when in closed position; a rotating body including a canal and a spout that enable to deviate the fluid flow laterally and upwards; a spout to enable the fluid flow to exit the present invention laterally and upwards; a head with a thread inside that enables the present invention to be mounted on an existing tap system. 14. The kit of parts according to claim 13 for making a tap head device according to the claims 1 to 12, the kit comprising: a body with a canal where the fluid passes through; a body to enable the fluid flow to exit the present invention vertically downwards when in closed position; an opening/closing valve for deviating the fluid flow laterally and upwards, instead of the rotating body including a canal and a spout; a spout to enable the fluid flow to exit the present invention laterally and upwards; a universal adapter and a screw to enable the installation of the present invention to an existing tap system, instead of the head with a thread inside.
